text:
SKYC Project  
Client requested for a strategic automation process through which customer data is regularly updated in adherence to Money Laundering Regulations.  
IBM worked with Client to set up a strategic case management lifecycle as part of the SKYC (Smart KYC) project to support an enduring CDD and ID&V journey, with flexibility to embed within the Event Based Monitoring (EBM) framework. As part of the project a Pega workflow management tool is being created to handle CDD and ID&V workflow.  
Results:  
- Automated process of data collection and case management for CDD and ID&V flows ensuring continued Regulatory compliance  
IBM’s Delivery team streamlined the project’s planning and execution to ensure smooth coordination between Business and technical stakeholders. The project also involved IBM Business Analysts, SIT, and Non-Functional teams who worked on the successful delivery of the project. The Power BI reports built by the team helped Business to check the remediation status.  
.co.uk Project  
The Customer Due Diligence (CDD) Remediation programme is designed to ensure compliance with Money Laundering Regulations (MLReg) obligations. It involves collecting missing CDD information and verifying that existing data is accurate and up-to-date. The existing approach captures customer details through Digital and Mediated channels.  
The image is a detailed flowchart illustrating a remediation process, likely for Customer Due Diligence (CDD) data.  
The process begins at the **CDD Cohort/FCCEP Remediation hub**. A key decision point asks: **"Does member have an existing mediated channel prompt"**?  
*   If the answer is **No**, the process moves to **"Invoke member on mediated channel"**.
*   If the answer is **Yes**, the process moves through steps involving **"Member outreach Phase 1: Email and/or Letter"** and providing **"With link to co.uk portal & unique reference number"**.  
Following these initial steps, the member performs actions such as **"Open DocuSign webform and enter URN & answer CDD questions"**. The process also acknowledges **"Other channels"** including **"Mediated Digital"**.  
The next step requires the member to complete a **"DocuSign form signed and submitted with personal & Customer CDD"**. The diagram notes that the **"DocuSign form is generic with no pre-populated fields"**.  
The process continues with **"Validate CDD Data provided"**.  
The final steps involve the **"RemOps team update data in CIS"**.  
Results:  
- The solution enhances customer experience by providing  an easy and alternative way for a customer to share information
- Improves Regulatory compliance  
To enhance client omni-channel strategy and improve customer engagement and completion rates, IBM is helping client build a new, reliable, and frictionless DocuSign solution on .co.uk. This alternative channel will allow client digitally inactive members to submit their CDD information without needing to visit a branch, use telephony services, or register for the Internet Bank or Mobile App. By broadening our approach, we aim to on increase overall participation and compliance through this journey.  
ID&V Project - ID Remediation  
The ID&V project involved enhancement of client ID&V solution to use an automated process to directly upload Customer ID cases that successfully passed data validations to CIS. The Society entered into an agreement with Mitek systems to use their Verified Identity Platform for this purpose. Any records which did not pass the data validation were sent to the exceptional workflow tool for subsequent manual updation by the Remediation Operation team through the SAMM application.  
This image is a flowchart detailing a process workflow.  
The process begins with **Mitek**, which feeds into a process diamond labeled **Data Validations**.  
From **Data Validations**, the process moves to the **ePaaS solution**, which is noted as **Fully automated (runs 24*7)**.  
*   If the data **Pass**es through the **ePaaS solution**, it proceeds to a component labeled **BIP**.
*   From **BIP**, an arrow points to a component labeled **CIS**.
*   **BIP** and **SAMM** are connected, and an annotation states that **BIP failures come via DSA to EWT**.  
*   If the process **Fails** at the **ePaaS solution**, it proceeds to **EWT**, which is contained within the **RemOps** section (indicated by a person icon).
*   **EWT** is shown to send a failure/notification path to **SAMM**.  
The overall flow describes the journey from **Mitek** through **Data Validations** and the **ePaaS solution**, handling both successful paths (**Pass** -> **BIP** -> **CIS**) and failure paths (**Fails** -> **EWT** -> **SAMM**), while also noting the relationship between **BIP**, **SAMM**, and **DSA**.  
Results:  
- Digital method of customer identification reduced workload at client branches
-  Increased convenience for overseas client customers
-  Streamlined operations and increased productivity of the Remediation Operation team  
IBM led the planning and execution of the project. Key requirements were gathered by IBM’s Business Analysts while the Development team validated the data between Mitek and CIS and also built an exception workflow tool for Operations.  Power BI reports built by the team enabled visibility into the remediation progress.  
Further, comprehensive testing by IBM’s SIT and Non-Functional teams ensured that both in-house and third-party components met quality, security, and performance standards for a smooth production rollout.  
CDD Project  
Results:  
- Reduced workload of client branches and Telephony by providing a digital process to collect customer data  
The Customer Due Diligence (CDD) project was initiated to ensure that the customer data stored in client applications was correct. Customer information was requested based on either the ‘Periodic refresh’ risk rating of the customer or on the basis of ‘Invoke refresh ‘ i.e., account behaviour. The customer data could be updated through digital channels like Internet banking or the Banking App or through Mediated channels such as Branch or Telephony.  
IBM’s Business Analysts were involved in Business requirement gathering for the project. The Data and Analytics team was involved in the following tasks:  
1. CDD invoke - to trigger the functionality to gather information from customer
2. CDD controls - to control the number of customer invoke to maintain the rate of customer request
3. CDD Revoke and Reinvoke - this was required to prevent digital restriction on the customer, if customer had not shared the required information within 90 days  
Business Savings Project (BuSa)  
The objective of this project was to establish a comprehensive and sustainable AML Remediation capability for Business Savings customers that ensured full compliance with the Financial Crime Minimum Control Standards  and the latest policy guidance. Client wanted to systematically address legacy compliance gaps by remediating the existing customer base through the collection and verification of missing Customer Due Diligence (CDD), and ID&V documentation.
name: Chunk 1/2

Customer information was requested based on either the ‘Periodic refresh’ risk rating... or on the basis of ‘Invoke refresh ‘ i.e., account behaviour.

entities.name: Continued regulatory compliance
entities.description: A quantified compliance outcome achieved through automated data collection and case management workflows. The benefit ensures that client operations consistently meet Money Laundering Regulations and Financial Crime Minimum Control Standards.
context: Automated process of data collection and case management for CDD and ID&V flows ensuring continued Regulatory compliance
Improves Regulatory compliance
ensured full compliance with the Financial Crime Minimum Control Standards

entities.name: Enhanced customer experience and engagement
entities.description: A qualified benefit resulting from the deployment of frictionless digital channels and alternative data submission pathways. The outcome improves customer satisfaction by providing easy, accessible methods for sharing required information without branch visits.
context: The solution enhances customer experience by providing an easy and alternative way for a customer to share information
To enhance client omni-channel strategy and improve customer engagement and completion rates...

entities.name: Reduced branch and telephony workload
entities.description: An operational efficiency benefit achieved by shifting customer data collection processes to digital and mediated channels. The outcome decreases dependency on physical branches and telephony services, lowering operational strain.
context: Digital method of customer identification reduced workload at client branches
Reduced workload of client branches and Telephony by providing a digital process to collect customer data

entities.name: Increased productivity and streamlined operations
entities.description: A performance benefit driven by automated workflows and comprehensive testing protocols that ensure smooth production rollouts. The outcome increases the efficiency of remediation operations teams while maintaining quality and security standards.
context: Streamlined operations and increased productivity of the Remediation Operation team
comprehensive testing by IBM’s SIT and Non-Functional teams ensured that both in-house and third-party components met quality, security, and performance standards

entities.name: Increased convenience for overseas customers
entities.description: A customer-centric benefit resulting from automated identity verification and digital data submission capabilities. The outcome provides seamless access to compliance processes for geographically dispersed or international client segments.
context: Increased convenience for overseas client customers
This alternative channel will allow client digitally inactive members to submit their CDD information without needing to visit a branch...

entities.name: Money Laundering Regulations compliance obligations
entities.description: A regulatory challenge requiring the client to maintain strict adherence to financial crime prevention standards. The obligation necessitates continuous data verification, periodic refreshes, and systematic remediation of customer records.
context: in adherence to Money Laundering Regulations.
ensure compliance with Money Laundering Regulations (MLReg) obligations.

entities.name: Legacy compliance gaps in customer data
entities.description: A data quality challenge stemming from outdated or incomplete customer records that require systematic remediation. The gap necessitates comprehensive data collection and verification processes to meet current policy guidance.
context: Client wanted to systematically address legacy compliance gaps by remediating the existing customer base...
ensure that the customer data stored in client applications was correct.

entities.name: Limited digital engagement channels
entities.description: An operational constraint where existing customer data collection relies heavily on traditional digital and mediated channels. The limitation restricts engagement for digitally inactive members and reduces overall compliance participation rates.
context: The existing approach captures customer details through Digital and Mediated channels.
This alternative channel will allow client digitally inactive members to submit their CDD information without needing to visit a branch...

entities.name: Data accuracy and up-to-date verification needs
entities.description: A functional challenge requiring continuous validation of customer information to maintain regulatory compliance. The need drives the implementation of automated refresh triggers and periodic data quality controls.
context: verifying that existing data is accurate and up-to-date.
CDD controls - to control the number of customer invoke to maintain the rate of customer request

entities.name: Pega workflow management tool
entities.description: A specialized automation platform deployed to manage Customer Due Diligence and Identity Verification workflows. The tool serves as the core engine for case management and strategic automation within the SKYC project.
context: As part of the project a Pega workflow management tool is being created to handle CDD and ID&V workflow.

entities.name: DocuSign
entities.description: A digital signature and document management platform integrated into the client’s omni-channel strategy. The technology enables frictionless data collection and secure information sharing for digitally inactive members.
context: IBM is helping client build a new, reliable, and frictionless DocuSign solution on .co.uk.
Open DocuSign webform and enter URN & answer CDD questions

entities.name: Mitek Verified Identity Platform
entities.description: A third-party identity verification system used to validate customer identification data. The platform feeds validated records into the client’s automated data validation and routing workflows.
context: The Society entered into an agreement with Mitek systems to use their Verified Identity Platform for this purpose.
The process begins with Mitek, which feeds into a process diamond labeled Data Validations.

entities.name: ePaaS solution
entities.description: A fully automated integration platform operating continuously to process and validate customer data. The system routes successful validations to core systems while managing failure notifications for exception handling.
context: From Data Validations, the process moves to the ePaaS solution, which is noted as Fully automated (runs 24*7).
The overall flow describes the journey from Mitek through Data Validations and the ePaaS solution...

entities.name: CIS (Customer Information System)
entities.description: A core database system used to store and update customer identification and due diligence records. The system serves as the final destination for successfully validated and uploaded customer data.
context: The RemOps team update data in CIS.
The Development team validated the data between Mitek and CIS...

entities.name: Digital and Mediated channels
entities.description: A set of customer interaction touchpoints including internet banking, mobile apps, branches, and telephony. The channels serve as primary data collection pathways for customer due diligence and identity verification processes.
context: The existing approach captures customer details through Digital and Mediated channels.
Customer information was requested based on either the ‘Periodic refresh’ risk rating... or through Mediated channels such as Branch or Telephony.

entities.name: Streamlined project planning and execution
entities.description: A delivery model differentiator that ensures smooth coordination between business and technical stakeholders. The approach minimizes project friction and accelerates time-to-value through structured planning and execution methodologies.
context: IBM’s Delivery team streamlined the project’s planning and execution to ensure smooth coordination between Business and technical stakeholders.

entities.name: Fully automated 24/7 processing capability
entities.description: A speed and reliability differentiator that enables continuous, unattended data validation and routing. The capability eliminates manual bottlenecks and ensures consistent processing throughput for customer identification workflows.
context: From Data Validations, the process moves to the ePaaS solution, which is noted as Fully automated (runs 24*7).

entities.name: Rigorous quality, security, and performance testing
entities.description: A certification and assurance differentiator that validates both in-house and third-party components against strict operational standards. The testing protocol ensures reliable production rollouts and mitigates technical and compliance risks.
context: comprehensive testing by IBM’s SIT and Non-Functional teams ensured that both in-house and third-party components met quality, security, and performance standards for a smooth production rollout.

entities.name: Frictionless omni-channel customer engagement
entities.description: An innovation differentiator that provides alternative, user-friendly data submission pathways for digitally inactive members. The capability broadens participation and improves compliance rates by removing traditional access barriers.
context: IBM is helping client build a new, reliable, and frictionless DocuSign solution on .co.uk.
By broadening our approach, we aim to on increase overall participation and compliance through this journey.

entities.name: IBM
entities.description: The primary vendor and solution provider delivering strategic automation, compliance remediation, and identity verification projects. IBM contributes delivery teams, business analysts, development resources, and testing capabilities to drive client outcomes.
context: IBM worked with Client to set up a strategic case management lifecycle...
IBM led the planning and execution of the project.
IBM’s Business Analysts were involved in Business requirement gathering...

entities.name: Mitek Systems
entities.description: A third-party technology partner providing identity verification platforms for customer identification and data validation. The organization’s solution integrates with the client’s automated workflows to support compliance and remediation objectives.
context: The Society entered into an agreement with Mitek systems to use their Verified Identity Platform for this purpose.

entities.name: SKYC Project
entities.description: A strategic automation initiative focused on establishing a case management lifecycle for Customer Due Diligence and Identity Verification. The project delivers workflow automation and regulatory compliance capabilities through integrated technology platforms.
context: SKYC Project
IBM worked with Client to set up a strategic case management lifecycle as part of the SKYC (Smart KYC) project...

entities.name: .co.uk Project
entities.description: A customer remediation programme designed to enhance compliance with Money Laundering Regulations through digital data collection. The project implements alternative channels and automated workflows to verify and update customer information.
context: .co.uk Project
The Customer Due Diligence (CDD) Remediation programme is designed to ensure compliance with Money Laundering Regulations (MLReg) obligations.

entities.name: ID&V Project
entities.description: An identity verification and remediation initiative that automates the upload and validation of customer ID cases. The project enhances operational efficiency by reducing branch workload and increasing convenience for overseas customers.
context: ID&V Project - ID Remediation
The ID&V project involved enhancement of client ID&V solution to use an automated process to directly upload Customer ID cases...

entities.name: CDD Project
entities.description: A Customer Due Diligence initiative focused on ensuring the accuracy of stored customer data through digital and mediated channels. The project implements automated triggers and controls to manage customer data refresh rates.
context: CDD Project
The Customer Due Diligence (CDD) project was initiated to ensure that the customer data stored in client applications was correct.

entities.name: Business Savings Project
entities.description: A compliance remediation programme targeting Business Savings customers to address legacy regulatory gaps. The project establishes sustainable AML capabilities to ensure adherence to Financial Crime Minimum Control Standards.
context: Business Savings Project (BuSa)
The objective of this project was to establish a comprehensive and sustainable AML Remediation capability for Business Savings customers...
